title: The Conductor's Orchestra
lede: You're not a player anymore. You're the conductor. Hand each instrument a part, set the tempo, listen for the wrong notes. The taste is yours. The hands are theirs.
frame: Kevin Friel's stack is chain thinking — Midjourney for frames, Runway for motion, ElevenLabs for audio, ComfyUI to glue, Topaz for polish. None of those play themselves into a finished piece. You do.

instruments.name: Frame Painter
instruments.task: Paint a moody establishing frame: rain-soaked Vancouver alley, sodium light, 35mm.
instruments.output: Generated 8 frames. Picked the third — soft blue cast, broken neon, puddle reflection lines up on the rule of thirds.

instruments.name: Motion Director
instruments.task: Push in 4 seconds on the alley. Subtle handheld, slight rain parallax.
instruments.output: Rendered 4s clip @ 24fps. Push-in feels intentional. Rain reads. Re-roll if you want stronger lens breathing.

instruments.name: Voice Actor
instruments.task: Read the line: "You don't need permission. You need a posse." Tired, intimate, no swagger.
instruments.output: Take 2 lands. Breath at the comma is a bit hot — would EQ a notch at 4k.

instruments.name: Scribe
instruments.task: Tighten the cold open from 90s to 60s. Keep the joke at 0:14. Cut all hedge words.
instruments.output: 60s draft attached. Cut 4 hedges, kept the joke, moved the thesis to 0:42. Reads cleaner.

instruments.name: Compositor
instruments.task: Comp the rain plate over the alley. Match black levels. Soft vignette, no halation.
instruments.output: Plate sits. Black levels match within 2%. Suggest a subtle grain pass at 50% to bind layers.

instruments.name: Polisher
instruments.task: Upres to 4K. Hold the grain. Don't smooth the wet brick texture.
instruments.output: 4K out. Grain preserved. Brick reads sharper than the source — the model hallucinated detail; flag for taste check.

closing.title: Same quality. Two hundred bucks. Two and a half days.
closing.body: Kevin Friel's line, not mine. The orchestra plays. The conductor decides what's worth keeping.
